title: peptide_macrocycle_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS G4 linear-vs-cyclic pre-organization comparison
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/peptide_macrocycle_cross.py. A CROSS-AXIS in-silico side-by-side of two pre-organization modalities across a peptide-length panel: (a) the LINEAR-PEPTIDE path (semaglutide-like; Zimm-Bragg helix-coil cooperative pre-organization via the PEPTIDE :> WEAVE sub-axis) and (b) the MACROCYCLE path (cyclosporine / lorlatinib-like; Jacobson-Stockmayer ring-closure pre-organization via the MACROCYCLE :> WEAVE sub-axis). CRITICAL HONESTY: this is a MODALITY COMPARISON of two in-silico model signatures — it is NOT an efficacy ranking, NOT a claim that one drug or modality is superior, NOT a clinical claim. semaglutide (Ozempic / Wegovy / Rybelsus, FDA 2017 s.c. / 2019 oral) and cyclosporine (FDA 1983) / lorlatinib (FDA 2018) are ALL FDA-approved blockbuster-scale therapeutics; linear peptides and macrocycles are BOTH widely-used drug modalities — one is not 'better.' The two model signals rest on different real limits (Zimm-Bragg 1-D helix-coil microstates vs Jacobson-Stockmayer rotamer-product microstates) and are not commensurable. Each modality is anchored to its own real limit and described by its own drug precedent; nothing is derived from the n=6 lattice (AGENTS.tape g1/g3/g8, f1/f2/f3, f_lattice_fit). CROSS != NEW AXIS — G4 is a cross over two existing sub-axes; core-5 (AXIS/AXIS.tape) is UNCHANGED. In-silico simulator-consistency only.

shared_biological_goal.description: The shared biological goal both modalities act toward (pre-organize the bound conformation of an N-residue pharmacophore). The comparison's common ground is the shared goal — NOT a shared metric.

shared_mechanism_kind.description: The shared mechanism kind both modalities exhibit (conformational entropy pre-payment), realised by physically different routes.

length_panel.description: Per-peptide-length cross rows: side-by-side LINEAR / MACROCYCLE signatures plus the linear-vs-cyclic entropic-advantage scalar at that N.

length_scaling_monotone.description: True iff the cyclization entropic advantage rises monotonically with peptide length N across the panel (each extra residue adds ~2 ring-constrained backbone bonds — the Jacobson-Stockmayer length scaling).

length_scaling_note.description: Human-readable note on the expected closed-form length scaling: advantage(N) ~ 2 * N * R * ln(1 / f_ring).

comparison_is_ranking.description: MUST be false. This deliverable is a descriptive side-by-side, never a ranking of the modalities or drugs.

comparison_kind.description: Human-readable description of the comparison kind (descriptive side-by-side over a length panel).

signals_commensurable.description: MUST be false. The LINEAR-PEPTIDE signal (Zimm-Bragg 2^N helix/coil microstate entropy) and the MACROCYCLE signal (rotamer-product ddS_cyc) rest on different real limits and are not commensurable.

signals_commensurable_note.description: Explanation of why the two signals are non-commensurable.

honesty.description: Mandatory honesty block — comparison is NOT a ranking and NOT a clinical/efficacy/superiority claim (g8/f2).

statement.description: Prose honesty statement asserting comparison != ranking, in-silico-only scope, both modalities FDA-approved, and CROSS != NEW AXIS.

length_row.description: One peptide-length cross row: side-by-side LINEAR and MACROCYCLE signatures at fixed residue count N, plus the linear-vs-cyclic entropic-advantage scalar.

n_residues.description: Number of residues in the model peptide (same N for both linear and macrocyclic analogs).

modality_rows.description: Exactly two modality signature rows for this N: LINEAR-PEPTIDE and MACROCYCLE.

linear_binding_entropy_penalty_kcal_per_.description: Conformational-entropy penalty the LINEAR peptide pays on freezing into the bound conformer (kcal/mol; >= 0). Derived from peptide_sim's Zimm-Bragg microstate accounting.

macrocycle_binding_entropy_penalty_kcal_.description: Conformational-entropy penalty the MACROCYCLE pays on freezing into the bound conformer (kcal/mol; >= 0). Derived from macrocycle_sim.conformational_entropy.

cyclization_entropic_advantage_kcal_per_.description: linear_binding_entropy_penalty - macrocycle_binding_entropy_penalty (kcal/mol). >= 0 by the Jacobson-Stockmayer real-limit floor (a ring can only REMOVE conformers). Magnitude only — NOT an efficacy delta and NOT a comparison of the two model signals against each other (they live on different real limits).

cyclization_advantage_is_favourable.description: True iff cyclization_entropic_advantage_kcal_per_mol >= 0 (Jacobson-Stockmayer hard ceiling: W_macro <= W_acyclic_with_same_2N_bonds).

expected_per_residue_advantage_kcal_per_.description: Closed-form expected per-residue length-scaling slope: 2 * R * T * ln(1 / f_ring) / KCAL_TO_J (kcal/mol per residue). Derived from the imported macrocycle_sim constants.

modality_row.description: One modality's in-silico model signature at fixed residue count N. The model_signal is anchored to that modality's OWN real limit and described via its OWN drug precedent — never lattice-derived. The two signals are intentionally on different real limits and are NOT commensurable across modalities.

modality.description: Which of the two pre-organization modalities this row describes.

axis_module.description: The imported sub-axis module that produced the model signal (no fork — governance f3).

axis_name.description: The hexa-bio sub-axis name (both modalities are :> WEAVE core).

drug_precedent.description: Own FDA-approved drug precedent for the modality (g3/f1 — described by precedent, never lattice-derived).

mechanism.description: Physical mechanism the modality realises (cooperative helix-coil folding vs ring-closure pre-organization).

n_residues.description: Number of residues in the model peptide for this row.

model_sequence.description: Deterministic model sequence used for the linear-peptide row (poly-Ala homopolymer of length n_residues).

n_rotatable_backbone_bonds.description: Number of backbone rotatable bonds for the macrocycle row (~ 2 * n_residues — phi, psi per residue).

ring_constraint_factor_f_ring.description: Macrocycle-only: f_ring in (0,1] from macrocycle_sim — in-ring effective rotamer multiplicity = f_ring * g_free.

rotamer_multiplicity_free.description: Macrocycle-only: free-bond rotamer multiplicity (~3 staggered rotamers for an sp3-sp3 bond).

sigma_nucleation.description: Linear-peptide-only: Zimm-Bragg helix-nucleation parameter sigma (<< 1 makes the helix-coil transition cooperative).

temperature_K.description: Temperature used for the -T * deltaS binding-entropy penalty (K).

model_signal_name.description: Name of the in-silico model-computed signal for this modality.

model_signal_value.description: The model-computed signal value. NOT comparable across modalities — different units, different real limits (signals_commensurable=false at the top level).

model_signal_detail.description: Modality-specific breakdown of the model signal (helix-coil partition for LINEAR-PEPTIDE; rotamer-product microstate accounting for MACROCYCLE).

real_limit_anchor.description: The real physical/statistical-mechanics limit this modality's signal is anchored to (g1 — real-limits-first).

real_limit_citations.description: Literature citations for the modality's real-limit anchor (Zimm-Bragg 1959 / Lifson-Roig 1961 for LINEAR-PEPTIDE; Jacobson-Stockmayer 1950 / Boltzmann 1877 for MACROCYCLE).

in_silico_only.description: MUST be true — in-silico simulator-consistency scope only (g8/f2).
